records.whySelected: Best used as a fast drafting and restructuring surface when the user already knows the question and needs speed more than deep provenance.
records.positioning: General-purpose thinking partner for outlining, rewriting, and turning messy notes into a first usable draft.
records.whenToUse: Use when you need fast iteration on wording, structure, or code-adjacent reasoning and you already have source material in hand.
howToUse: Start with the task, audience, and output format instead of asking for general help.
Paste only the relevant excerpts, then ask for one concrete transformation at a time.
Run a separate verification pass for citations, numerical claims, and quoted language.
limitations: Can sound confident while inventing details.
Not a substitute for citation verification or journal-specific compliance checks.
alternatives: Paperpal for language polishing inside an academic writing workflow
avoidIf: you want manuscript-ready references without manual checking

records.whySelected: Useful when the work depends on holding large context windows together: long manuscripts, review comments, appendices, and code-adjacent documentation.
records.positioning: Long-context reviewer for synthesis, critique, and revision planning across large source bundles.
records.whenToUse: Use when context continuity matters more than quick conversational speed.
howToUse: Upload the manuscript, reviewer notes, and any constraints in one pass.
Ask for a structured diagnosis before requesting rewritten text.
Break final edits into section-level passes once the revision plan is stable.
limitations: Still requires fact-checking and reference verification.
Can overproduce polished prose when what you need is a strict decision memo.
avoidIf: the task is a quick wording tweak
you need an integrated academic language checker

records.whySelected: Strong fit for moving from a question to a structured evidence table without manually opening dozens of papers first.
records.positioning: Evidence-mapping tool for narrowing a literature question into comparable studies and extracted fields.
suitableFor: question refinement before a formal review
records.whenToUse: Use when the goal is to compare papers systematically rather than simply read one paper deeply.
howToUse: Phrase the query as a research question, not a keyword bag.
Inspect the retrieved papers and prune the obvious mismatches early.
Export or manually transfer the extracted fields into your own review sheet.
limitations: Coverage and extraction quality still need manual review.
Not enough on its own for a publishable systematic review workflow.
alternatives: Semantic Scholar for broader search and citation graphing
Consensus for quick evidence-oriented question answering
avoidIf: you already know the exact paper set

records.whySelected: Good at helping users get through difficult PDFs faster, especially when terminology and methods are slowing down comprehension.
records.positioning: Interactive paper-reading layer for unpacking dense papers before you decide whether they belong in the serious shortlist.
records.whenToUse: Use when reading friction is the bottleneck and you need a guided first pass through a paper.
howToUse: Upload one paper at a time when the goal is close reading.
Ask targeted questions about methods, assumptions, and limitations.
Capture your own notes separately so the paper remains usable outside the tool.
limitations: Answers can flatten nuance from complex methods sections.
Not designed to replace your own citation or claim verification.
alternatives: Kimi for long-context comparison across multiple PDFs
ChatGPT for custom note transformation after reading
avoidIf: you need a corpus-level search workflow
the paper must be reviewed with exact quotations only

records.whySelected: Worth featuring when the station wants to show how AI tools move from chat into repo-level execution and review.
records.positioning: Research coding environment for iterative implementation, refactoring, and code-aware assistant loops.
suitableFor: analysis notebooks graduating into maintainable code
records.whenToUse: Use when the task depends on understanding multiple files and making changes with project context.
howToUse: Open the narrowest folder that still contains the real context.
Ask for inspection and constraints before asking for edits.
Keep tests or validation commands ready and treat generated changes as reviewable diffs.
limitations: Can spread mistakes across multiple files quickly if the initial instruction is vague.
Still needs human review for architecture and correctness.
alternatives: GitHub Copilot for inline completion inside an existing editor workflow
ChatGPT for isolated code explanation and planning
avoidIf: the task is a single short snippet
you cannot verify the resulting code

records.whySelected: Featured as a specialist recommendation: expensive for casual use, but hard to replace when the figure must communicate cleanly in a paper or presentation.
records.positioning: Specialist figure-production tool for researchers who need polished biological or scientific schematics quickly.
records.whenToUse: Use when visual clarity and professional scientific iconography matter more than full design flexibility.
howToUse: Start from an existing template close to your mechanism or workflow.
Reduce the figure to one claim per panel before polishing style.
Export a draft early and check whether the figure still reads outside the editor.
limitations: Less suitable for generic product diagrams or highly custom layouts.
Subscription cost is hard to justify for occasional figure work.
alternatives: Adobe Illustrator for fully custom publication graphics
avoidIf: you only need a rough workflow box diagram
